title: Express Middleware, Promise Errors, Dependencies, and Response Ownership · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order.
Use Express 5 automatic Promise rejection forwarding while preserving causal error identity and avoiding duplicate next calls.
Implement four-argument error middleware, normalized failure taxonomy, disclosure policy, and headersSent delegation without double writes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Middleware Order Short Circuit
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 5429 in a accessible status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to trace defended middleware order while keeping error and response ownership explicit; reject a replay before any external effect; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order.
